sections.title: Talking with Launa before going down

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Have you discovered anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, what am I supposed to do again?

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Go into the rift beside me and find my husband and son!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, I'm on my way!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's this big hole in the ground all about?

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Oh I wish I knew for sure... All I do know is that it opened seemingly by itself a few months ago. It was first discovered by the students over at the digsite, a few of which have gone down there but never returned.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: They told me about it straight away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why is it important to you?

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: A few reasons, but I think the situation is beyond help now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What situation? Maybe I can help.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: I suppose it wouldn't hurt to tell you. I'm waiting to see my son Tolna. Don't suppose you've seen him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your son, Tolna? I don't know, what does he look like?

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: I don't really know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't know what your own son looks like?

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: No. You see, I haven't seen him for... for... 25 years now. He just ran away one day and never returned. I'm hoping he's down this rift as I know he headed in this direction. My husband went to look, but he hasn't

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: returned for days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you like me to go down to look for your husband and son?

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: You would do that? Oh thank you so much!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry. I'll investigate.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Thank you. You can use a rope to get into the rift, just attach it at the edge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Be careful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Surely by now they'd both be dead?

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: What?!?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oops.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: How could you say such a thing? Get away from me!

sections.title: Talking to Launa after entering the room of fear

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I've been down into the rift and I found a strange room. I also heard a voice claiming to be called Tolna.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: My son! What did he say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He said, 'Why should I, Tolna, be trapped in such a wretched place? Feel my ANGER!'.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Who can be surprised at his frustration?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a very aggressive room. I think I need to defeat the monsters in there.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Don't waste any time, his life could depend on you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arrrgghhh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow! How did I do that? There's something truly mental about this place!

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Where are you going?

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: I can't live with your rules any more, Mother.

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: I'm tired of working hard for very little money. There's great wealth that's mine for the taking!

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: But why? We're surviving perfectly fine on what we're earning.

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: I'm tired of your pathetic outlook. Good bye.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Tolna?

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: TOLNA !!!

sections.title: Talking to Launa after watching the cutscene of Tolna leaving

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I managed to defeat the monsters in the room!

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Have you found him then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I've yet to enter the next room.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Please hurry!

lines.speaker: Tolna's Voice
lines.text: Why should I, Tolna, be trapped in such a wretched place?

lines.speaker: Tolna's Voice
lines.text: Arrghh, It's not fair! Feel my ANGER!

sections.title: Talking to Launa after entering the room of fear

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi. I've made my way into a second room. I heard your son's voice again as well.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: What did he say? What did he say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'It's so quiet and dark. I FEAR I may never escape this horrid place. Mum, help me!'

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: You must save him! Help him get over his fear!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm, no light sources. So why can I still see down here? This can't just be a simple dungeon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What on Gielinor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahhhh

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Help me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh no!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who's there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What on Gielinor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is anybody there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't see!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What on Gielinor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who's that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What on Gielinor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't like looking in these holes but I must!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't feel quite so afraid now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: These holes aren't that spooky.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This isn't so scary after all!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Phew, I don't feel so scared now. This feels like the figment of someone's imagination!

sections.title: Talking to Launa after completing the room of fear

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've overcome fear in the second room and an exit has been illuminated.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: What can you see?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm, I haven't actually ventured through yet.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: What are you waiting for? He must be so afraid in that place.

lines.speaker: Tolna's Voice
lines.text: It's so quiet and dark. I FEAR I may never escape this horrid place.

lines.speaker: Tolna's Voice
lines.text: Mum, Dad, help me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm, no light sources. So why can I still see down here? This can't just be a simple dungeon.

sections.title: Talking to Launa after entering the room of confusion

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I've journeyed through to a third room. Your son's voice spoke as I entered.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Really? What did he say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Simply, 'Where am I? How long have I been here? Am I still the person I once was? I hate my CONFUSION'.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: You have to help him overcome his confusion!

sections.title: Killing the last confusion beast each time

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aha! The exit is now clear! This Tolna's mind really is a mess.

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: Leave me... But I don't want to... How can you say that...

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: I've done nothing wrong... Arrrghhh...

sections.title: Talking to Launa after completing the confusion room

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi. I have prevailed over a third room.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Are you any closer to finding my son?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I may be getting closer.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Please don't waste any time.

lines.speaker: Tolna's Voice
lines.text: Where am I? How long have I been here? Am I still the person I once was?

lines.speaker: Tolna's Voice
lines.text: Oh why am I so CONFUSED?

sections.title: Talking to Launa after entering the room of hopelessness

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have travelled through to a fourth room. As I entered your son's voice spoke to me!

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Oh my! What did he say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He said, 'What's the point? Why do I defy such HOPELESSNESS? No-one will ever help me'.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: You must fight to give him back his hope!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I succeed?

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Of course you can!

sections.title: After killing the first hopeless creature

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there any end to killing these monsters?

sections.title: After killing the second hopeless creature

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is hard work!

sections.title: After killing the third hopeless creature

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've almost killed them all now!

sections.title: After killing the fourth hopeless creature

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe if I kill one more?

sections.title: After killing the last hopeless creature

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A bridge of hope! Tolna should feel that help is at hand by now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have endured against hopelessness to finish the fourth room.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Any sight of my son?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Only his voice. So far he has said to me, 'Why should I, Tolna, be trapped in such a wretched place? Feel my ANGER!', 'It's so quiet and dark. I FEAR I may never escape this horrid place. Mum, help me!', 'Where

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: am I? How long have I been here? Am I still the person I once was? I hate my CONFUSION' and 'What's the point? Why do I defy such HOPELESSNESS? No-one will ever help me'.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: You must find him and bring him back to me. Please, I beg you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I will find him!

lines.speaker: Tolna's Voice
lines.text: What's the point? No-one will ever help me.

lines.speaker: Tolna's Voice
lines.text: I'm not worth anything. Will anyone ever combat my HOPELESSNESS?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you ok?

lines.speaker: Brana
lines.text: Please! You must help my son! He's beyond sense.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But how can I help?

lines.speaker: Brana
lines.text: I take it you have travelled through the mental dilemmas of my son, as I have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's correct.

lines.speaker: Brana
lines.text: Now you must do what I cannot, conquer the physical dilemma!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will try.

lines.speaker: Brana
lines.text: Thank you stranger.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found your son.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: You have?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And your husband, but I'm afraid your son is no longer physically what he once was.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Can you save them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will try.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: Thank you.

sections.title: Talking to Brana after defeating Tolna's body

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've saved your son.

lines.speaker: Brana
lines.text: Please talk to him, make sure he's OK!

sections.title: Talking to Launa after defeating Tolna's body

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have returned your son to his normal state!

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: And you've spoken to him?!?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er, no, sorry. I just came back here to tell you.

lines.speaker: Launa
lines.text: What are you waiting for, bring him back to me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tolna?

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: Sorry? What happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've been trapped down here for years. I've come to rescue you on behalf of your parents.

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: Oh yeah. I was looking for the legacy of a civilisation that used to live down here.

lines.speaker: Brana
lines.text: Son!

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: Father? Oh, I'm so sorry, can you ever forgive me?

lines.speaker: Brana
lines.text: Don't worry, I'm just glad you're back to yourself. Are you OK?

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: I'm fine, my memories are slowly returning. How's mother?

lines.speaker: Brana
lines.text: She misses you dearly!

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: Where is she?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She's up at the surface waiting for you.

lines.speaker: Brana
lines.text: Thank you traveller, I'm glad my wife and I never gave up hope. We should return to her and tell her the good news.

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: Thank you so much. I truly owe you for your help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just glad I could help.

lines.speaker: Brana
lines.text: Son! Is that you?

lines.speaker: Brana
lines.text: Please speak to me! Tolna?

lines.speaker: Brana
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Brana
lines.text: You'll always be my son!

lines.speaker: Brana
lines.text: There must be a way.

lines.speaker: Brana
lines.text: Don't give up so easily!

lines.speaker: Brana
lines.text: Sorry, but your mother and I...

lines.speaker: Brana
lines.text: tried so hard to find you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your father isn't lying.

sections.title: Talking to Tolna on the surface

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Tolna.

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: Hi. Thanks again for saving me, I'm so glad to be out of that horrible place. I was foolish to ignore my parents' warnings when I ran away that day. Luckily I've been able to patch things up with them so they've

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: returned home with peace of mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly happened to you down there?

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: I managed to find the remnants of the ancient civilisation that the stories talked about. It seems people used to worship a god there years ago in numbers far greater than a simple cult following. Sadly there is little

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: remaining so I have nothing else to tell. As soon as I entered this area the ground swallowed me whole. I felt there was something watching, waiting. It's like there is some evil power under the ground that wanted me to

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: suffer. As if it got some sort of empowerment from it. At least what ever it is, it doesn't want to reveal itself to the world yet...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: At least we have a warning. Did you manage to retrieve anything?

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: Not really, just a few coins, which you're welcome to have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. So what are you going to do now?

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: I've decided to keep open the rift. There is a part of it that is perfectly laid out for combat training and remnants of my subconscious remain in there. I want to show people the problems I have faced, so that they

lines.speaker: Tolna
lines.text: may learn from them. You're welcome to go down there and practice fighting any time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.
